title: Interview with Bryan Magee - Philosophy and Literature | Listen Notes
text: EPISODE COMMUNITY EMBED EDIT ## ABOUT THIS EPISODE [](https://cdn-images-3.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/interview-with-bryan-magee-btNryFLjzdu-vw9Fo5K4D3L.1400x1400.jpg "Interview with Bryan Magee - Philosophy and Literature") In this interview, first broadcast on the BBC in 1977, Murdoch discusses her work in both philosophy and literature with Bryan Magee. An excellent companion piece, by the Murdochian scholar Gillian Dooley, can be found here: [Fiction](https://www.listennotes.com/best-fiction-podcasts-168/ "View the best Fiction podcasts") English United Kingdom Use API to fetch this episode in JSON ## IN THIS EPISODE \# bryan magee [](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bryan_Magee "View Wikipedia page about bryan magee") [Edit these tags](https://www.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/interview-with-bryan-magee-vw9Fo5K4D3L/) ## TRANSCRIPT 🔗 Are you the producer of this podcast? Embed this search bar to your website ## OTHER EPISODES IN THIS PODCAST [](https://cdn-images-3.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/iris-murdoch-birthday-pLw2730eOdH-Md8FN056sRu.1400x1400.jpg "Iris Murdoch Birthday Lecture 2025") ### [Iris Murdoch Birthday Lecture 2025](https://www.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/iris-murdoch-birthday-Md8FN056sRu/ "Iris Murdoch Birthday Lecture 2025") July 16, 2025 In this lecture Miles Leeson, Director of the Iris Murdoch Research Centre at the University of Chichester, discusses Murdoch’s reception by her contemporaries and look at the lighter side of how she was lampooned, both directly and indirectly, in the work of H.E. Bates, Malcolm Bradbury, Brigid Br… 00:47:45 SHARE SAVE CLIP [](https://cdn-images-3.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/poems-from-an-attic-podcast-FSBgRd45Dxi-JwkXU6TVxzA.1400x1400.jpg "Poems from an Attic Podcast") ### [Poems from an Attic Podcast](https://www.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/poems-from-an-attic-podcast-JwkXU6TVxzA/ "Poems from an Attic Podcast") Nov. 6, 2025 In this episode Miles is joined by Anne Rowe (University of Chichester/Kingston), Rachel Hirschler (Kinston University) and Rosanna Hilyard (Chatto & Windus) to celebrate the publication of 'Poems from An Attic: Selected Poems 1936-1995', published today by Chatto and Windus. https://www.pengu… 01:03:14 SHARE SAVE CLIP [](https://cdn-images-3.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/iris-murdoch-and-early-V7zdQWvwips-rJR27PthBTX.1400x1400.jpg "Iris Murdoch and Early Childhood Education Podcast") ### [Iris Murdoch and Early Childhood Education Podcast](https://www.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/iris-murdoch-and-early-rJR27PthBTX/ "Iris Murdoch and Early Childhood Education Podcast") Oct. 30, 2025 In this episode Miles talks to Andrea Delaune (University of Canterbury, New Zealand) about her new book, 'Iris Murdoch and Early Childhood Education: Enhancing Attention and Moral Vision in Pedagogy' (Routledge, 2025). https://www.routledge.com/Iris-Murdoch-and-Early-Childhood-Education-Enhancin… 00:57:36 SHARE SAVE CLIP [](https://cdn-images-3.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/iris-murdoch-and-the-virtues-ITdrRoyVX-i-IM1if0ZPxUH.1400x1400.jpg "Iris Murdoch and the Virtues") ### [Iris Murdoch and the Virtues](https://www.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/iris-murdoch-and-the-virtues-IM1if0ZPxUH/ "Iris Murdoch and the Virtues") Sept. 15, 2025 In this episode we discuss Murdoch’s conceptualisation of virtue and what it might mean to be virtuous. We’ll range across her philosophy, of course, but we’ll also have time to visit her fiction and consider if she embeds some of her ideas about virtue into her novels. Joining Miles to discuss th… 01:03:25 SHARE SAVE CLIP [](https://cdn-images-3.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/a-word-child-revisited-podcast-u-kCmXHLEyr-E5aQCXJxhbS.1400x1400.jpg "A Word Child Revisited Podcast") ### [A Word Child Revisited Podcast](https://www.listennotes.com/podcasts/the-iris-murdoch/a-word-child-revisited-podcast-E5aQCXJxhbS/ "A Word Child Revisited Podcast") Sept. 1, 2025 Welcome to a new season of the Iris Murdoch Podcast! In this episode we’re celebrating the 50th anniversary of the publication of one of Murdoch very best novels, and one of the six first-person male narrated novels, A Word Child.
